gcc_integration_status.relationship: Jordan is a GCC member, part of Customs Union
gcc_integration_status.tariff_basis: Based on GCC Common External Tariff (CET)
gcc_integration_status.harmonization: Moving to unified 12-digit system with other GCC states
gcc_integration_status.transition_deadline: January 1, 2026 - all shipments must use 12-digit codes

commodity_sections.coverage: All product categories from animals to manufactured goods

important_notes: Jordan currently uses 10-digit codes (will transition to 12-digit January 1, 2026)
Part of GCC Customs Union with coordinated tariff structure
JCAP portal is the official government source
Both Arabic and English versions available
Tariff codes align with GCC Common External Tariff (CET)
Preferential rates available for regional trade partners
Exemptions apply for certain goods and origins

transition_implications.requirement: All commercial shipments must use 12-digit HS codes
transition_implications.impact: Businesses must update their coding systems before deadline
transition_implications.note: Compatible with other GCC member transitions to 12-digit system

bulk_download_status.complete_database: Not explicitly mentioned; may require portal download of all 22 sections
bulk_download_status.recommendation: Contact Jordan Customs for bulk data export requests

limitations: No direct mention of complete CSV/Excel download
Section-by-section download required for comprehensive data
Portal interface is primary access method
Some data may require direct contact with customs authority
Transition period may cause data version confusion (10-digit vs 12-digit)
recommended_action: Verify 12-digit format availability on JCAP portal as of January 1, 2026; current portal may still show 10-digit codes
